q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is HTMLResponse(body) which constructs a response object in memory, not network I/O, shell execution, or irreversible side effects

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The XSS violation (unescaped user input in HTML) is directly present in the returned HTMLResponse object

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' with exprs ['HTMLResponse(body)'], so the response object containing unescaped HTML is directly accessible in memory on return
